events.label: assistant
events.text: Findings written to `/app/answer.json`. Bottom line: a single-pole AFCI on a shared-neutral (MWBC) circuit will nuisance-trip by design — the unbalanced neutral current is misread as an arc fault. The sequence violation is troubleshooting/swapping the breaker before de-energizing and confirming the shared-neutral condition; the root wiring fix (separate dedicated neutrals or a listed two-pole common-trip AFCI, per NEC 210.8/210.12 and manufacturer instructions) must happen before re-energizing and retesting.
